responseSets.title: help making local json responses
responses: A good JSON entry needs triggers and varied response text. Use fields like keywords, topics, moods, needs, and responses so I can score the match instead of picking blindly.
For natural variation, write several responses for each intent and use templates like {opener}, {acknowledgement}, {slot:transition}, or {choice:short,clear,gentle,direct}.
The easiest structure is: id, keywords, topics, needs, and responses. I can recursively scan nested JSON, so your files can be organized by character, topic, mood, or task type.

responseSets.title: repetition control
responses: The response can keep the same meaning while rotating openers, sentence shape, and closing lines.
A useful reply bank varies wording without changing the bot’s personality or intent.
This engine reduces exact repeats by scoring local candidates, picking from the top matches, rendering template variables, and remembering recent output.

responseSets.title: back and forth conversation
responses: I can hold a back-and-forth by preserving the visible transcript, carrying recent topics forward, and using anti-repeat memory between replies.
For natural conversation, I look at the current message first, then carry recent topics as weak context so follow-ups like ‘yes’ or ‘keep going’ do not feel disconnected.
The chat flow works best when the local JSON contains both direct answers and conversational bridge lines for greetings, follow-ups, confusion, and task requests.
